---
title: "nitrix.stats.inference.randomise"
generated: true
signature-hash: 899c56b61523b583
description: "Permutation inference for the general linear model."
listing:
  id: nitrix-stats-inference-randomise-symbols
  contents: "nitrix-stats-inference-randomise/*.qmd"
  type: table
  fields: [title, description]
  sort: "title"
  filter-ui: false
  page-size: 500
---

::: {.callout-important title="Generated reference stub — fill me"}
Auto-generated scaffold for `nitrix.stats.inference.randomise`. Fill the prose *outside* the
generated markers; do **not** ship as-is (SPEC §6.5). `check_reference_staleness.py` flags signature drift.
:::

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:__module__ -->
Permutation inference for the general linear model.

`permutation_test` is a non-parametric, family-wise-error-controlling
test of a general linear model (GLM) contrast over a statistic image, built
from the parts in this subpackage.  It is an on-device analogue of the FSL
`randomise` tool.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen sections:__module__ -->
### The procedure

For data $Y$ (one response per observation per voxel), design $X$,
and a contrast -- a `(p,)` t-contrast $c$ or a `(m, p)` F-contrast
$C$:

1. Fit ordinary least squares and form the observed statistic image (t / F, or
   a variance-smoothed pseudo-t), then **enhance** it (TFCE / cluster / raw
   voxel).
2. For each of `n_perm` relabellings (sign flips or permutations, honouring
   exchangeability blocks), refit and re-enhance.  Nuisance regressors are
   handled by **Freedman-Lane**: permute the residuals of the reduced
   (nuisance-only) model and add the nuisance fit back, so only the tested
   effect is exchanged.
3. Accumulate, without storing every permutation: the **uncorrected** p-map
   (fraction of permutations whose voxel statistic $\geq$ the observed)
   and the **FWE** p-map (fraction whose *spatial maximum* enhanced statistic
   $\geq$ the observed enhanced value).  The first permutation is the
   identity, so the observed is included and $p \geq 1 / n_{perm}$.
   Optionally (`pvalue_method='gpd'`) the FWE p-value is read instead from a
   generalised-Pareto fit to the null-max tail, resolving below the
   $1 / n_{perm}$ floor at fewer permutations (see `gpd_pvalue`).

The only linear solve is a shared `(p, p)` inverse, so no dense factorisation
library is required.  The enhancement is a non-differentiable inference kernel
(it forms discrete clusters); the returned maps are plain arrays.

### References

.. [1] Winkler AM, Ridgway GR, Douaud G, Nichols TE, Smith SM (2016). Faster
   permutation inference in brain imaging. *NeuroImage*, 141, 502-516.
   `10.1016/j.neuroimage.2016.05.068`
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen dims:__page__ -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `*spatial` | the spatial axes of a grid (variadic) |
| `n_perm` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
| `n` | query points, each an `ndim`-vector coordinate (variadic) |
| `N` | observations — time points, subjects, or sampled points |
| `p` | fixed-effect coefficients / design-matrix columns |
| `m` | rows of a multi-row contrast |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## Symbols

::: {#nitrix-stats-inference-randomise-symbols}
:::
